title: Fix 'Unauthorized — admin only' error for authenticated admin users

requirements.text: Fix the backend admin role check so that when a user registered with the 'admin' role calls privileged functions (such as addFeeItem, addCourse, registerStudent, updateAdmissionStatus, postResult, sendAnnouncement, approveHostelApplication, etc.), the caller's principal is correctly matched against the stored admin profile. The role check must read from the same stable profiles store used during registration and must not return #err("Unauthorized — admin only") for a principal that has a stored profile with role = #admin.

quotes: Each time I try to add fee or any other thing they usually says unauthorized only admin allowed but I registered as an admin

acceptanceCriteria: An authenticated user whose stored profile has role = #admin can call addFeeItem without receiving an 'Unauthorized' error.
An authenticated user whose stored profile has role = #admin can call addCourse without receiving an 'Unauthorized' error.
An authenticated user whose stored profile has role = #admin can call updateAdmissionStatus, postResult, sendAnnouncement, and approveHostelApplication without receiving an 'Unauthorized' error.
The backend helper function that checks for admin role uses the same stable HashMap/Trie/variable that profiles are written to during registration — not a separate or uninitialized store.
A user with a non-admin role (student, staff, parent) still receives the 'Unauthorized' error when attempting admin-only functions.

After the fix, the Admin Panel FeeManagementPage, ProgrammeManagementPage, StudentManagementPage, ResultsManagementPage, MessagingPage, and HostelManagementPage all complete their operations successfully without surfacing an authorization error.

requirements.text: Audit all admin-gated backend functions in main.mo and consolidate the admin authorization check into a single shared helper (e.g., isAdmin(caller: Principal): Bool) that reads from the stable profiles store. Replace any ad-hoc or duplicated role checks throughout the file with calls to this helper to ensure consistency.

quotes: Each time I try to add fee or any other thing they usually says unauthorized only admin allowed but I registered as an admin

acceptanceCriteria: A single isAdmin (or equivalent) private helper function exists in main.mo and is used by every admin-gated function.
The helper correctly looks up the caller's principal in the stable profiles store and returns true only if the stored role is #admin.
No admin-gated function contains its own inline role-check logic that could diverge from the central helper.
All previously broken admin operations (fee creation, course creation, student registration, result posting, messaging, hostel approvals) succeed after this consolidation.

requirements.text: In the frontend Admin Panel pages (FeeManagementPage, ProgrammeManagementPage, StudentManagementPage, ResultsManagementPage, MessagingPage, HostelManagementPage), surface a clear, human-readable error toast or inline message when a backend mutation returns an authorization error, so admins know immediately if the session/actor has expired and they need to re-authenticate, rather than silently failing or showing a raw error string.

quotes: Each time I try to add fee or any other thing they usually says unauthorized only admin allowed but I registered as an admin

acceptanceCriteria: When any admin mutation fails with an authorization-related error, a visible toast or error banner is shown with text like 'Action failed: you must be logged in as an Admin. Please re-authenticate and try again.'
The error state is cleared when the user retries or navigates away.
Successful mutations show a success confirmation as before.

constraints: Do not change the stable data schema for user profiles — only fix the role-check logic that reads from it.
Do not modify files listed under frontend.immutablePaths.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single main actor in backend/main.mo.
nonGoals: Redesigning the profile registration flow or the ProfileSetupModal UI.
Adding new admin features beyond fixing the authorization bug.
Changing how Internet Identity authentication itself works.
